disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Abrupt, maximal-intensity tearing/ripping pain between the shoulder blades, reaching peak instantly
trigger_question_examples: Where exactly is the pain and what does it feel like?
Did it come on suddenly or build up gradually?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It hit me all at once between my shoulder blades — a tearing, ripping feeling, like being torn open. It was at its very worst from the first second.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain is migrating caudally over time — from the back down into the abdomen, flank and legs
trigger_question_examples: Has the pain moved or stayed in one place?
Where is the pain now compared with when it started?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started in my back but it's been travelling down — now it's ripping into my stomach and my left side and down my legs.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: New unilateral limb symptom — the left leg is weak, numb, cold and 'dead' feeling with pallor
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any weakness, numbness or coldness anywhere?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My left leg has gone numb and weak and it feels cold and dead — it looks paler than the other one and I can't really move it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known severe, poorly-controlled hypertension with recent non-adherence to antihypertensives
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any medical conditions such as high blood pressure?
Are you taking any regular medications?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My blood pressure has always been high and hard to control. I'm meant to be on tablets but I ran out weeks ago and haven't taken them.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Nausea and a sense of being profoundly unwell, diaphoretic
trigger_question_examples: Do you feel sick or sweaty?
How unwell do you feel in yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I feel really sick in the stomach and drenched in sweat — I've never felt this unwell.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain is not reproduced or changed by pressing on the back or by position — argues against a musculoskeletal cause
trigger_question_examples: Does pressing on your back or changing position make the pain better or worse?
Did you injure or strain your back?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I didn't hurt it and pressing on my back doesn't change it — this isn't like a pulled muscle, it's deep and tearing.
